title: Rosehip and roman chamomile balm
subtitle: A gentle, fragrant balm with rosehip oil, shea butter, beeswax, and roman chamomile essential oil
excerpt: This balm combines the skin-nourishing properties of rosehip oil and shea butter with beeswax for structure and roman chamomile essential oil for its characteristic apple-sweet, calming scent. The formula is waterless, so no preservative is needed. It suits dry, irritated, or sensitive skin on the face and body. Yield is approximately 95 g in a salve tin.
sourceNotes: Balm formula: beeswax at 20% of total weight gives a firm but spreadable set. Shea butter (Vitellaria paradoxa): rich in oleic acid, linoleic acid, and vitamin A; well-tolerated by most skin types including sensitive. Rosehip oil: vitamin A precursor, linoleic acid; oxidises faster than shea alone, but at 25% of the formula the overall blend is reasonably stable. Roman chamomile EO (Anthemis nobilis) at 0.7% of total weight: IFRA 49th amendment leave-on limit for a face product is 0.7%; this rate is used here to ensure the balm is suitable for both face and body use without exceeding the facial leave-on limit.

recipe.yieldDescription: Approximately 95 g in a salve tin
recipe.batchNotes: Scale by multiplying all weights. One salve tin per batch.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Sets within 1 hour at room temperature. Ready to use once solid.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a cool, dark place. Rosehip oil oxidises faster than shea butter; the blend should remain good for 12 months with the antioxidant protection from the shea. Discard if the balm develops a rancid or paint-like smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: A preparation containing no water phase. Anhydrous formulations do not require a preservative because bacteria and mould cannot grow without water. Balms, salves, body butters, and solid lotion bars are all anhydrous.

content.text: preparation: no water, no emulsifier, no preservative. Beeswax at 20% of the total weight gives the balm a firm-but-spreadable set that softens on contact with skin warmth. Shea butter contributes oleic acid and a smooth, creamy skin feel; rosehip oil adds linoleic acid and beta-carotene. Roman chamomile essential oil brings its apple-sweet, gently floral scent. This balm is mild enough for face or body; the essential oil rate is kept low to stay within leave-on facial limits. Patch test before applying to the face, particularly on reactive skin.

items.prepNote: Cosmetic-grade beeswax; provides the firm set
Unrefined or refined; provides emollient base
Use fresh oil; rosehip oxidises relatively quickly
0.7% of total weight; apple-sweet, floral; add at 50°C

content.text: Place the beeswax pellets and shea butter in a heatproof bowl over a pan of barely simmering water. Stir occasionally until both are fully melted. Remove from the heat. Add the rosehip oil and stir to combine; the cooler rosehip oil will bring the temperature down.
Add the essential oil and pour

Once the mixture has cooled to around 50°C, add the roman chamomile essential oil and stir well. Pour immediately into the salve tin while still liquid; the beeswax will begin to set as it cools. Pour in one continuous motion to avoid layering. Leave undisturbed until fully solid, about 1 hour.

Store in the lidded tin in a cool, dark place. The balm is stable for 12 months. Rosehip oil has a shorter shelf life than most carriers; discard if the balm develops a rancid or paint-like smell. Apply a small amount with a clean fingertip to face or body as needed.
